entities.name: Selftest (Calibration Wizard)
entities.description: Initial calibration and testing wizard that runs on first power-on of the MK4S printer.

entities.name: Print Sheet
entities.description: Print surface that must be installed on the printer before running the calibration wizard.

entities.name: Internet Setup Configuration
entities.description: Optional step in the Selftest wizard to connect the printer to the network.

entities.name: LCD Touchscreen
entities.description: The printer's touchscreen display used to show wizard prompts and menus.

entities.name: Run Full Selftest
entities.description: Manual menu command (LCD menu - Control - Run Full Selftest) to start the Selftest wizard again.

entities.name: LCD Menu - Control
entities.description: Menu location on the LCD interface where manual selftest can be triggered.

entities.name: Assembly Issues
entities.description: Basic assembly issues that the Selftest checks for on kit-built printers.

entities.name: Shipping Damage Issues
entities.description: Issues potentially caused by rough handling during shipping, identified by the Selftest on pre-assembled printers.

entities.name: Toolhead
entities.description: Assembly housing hotend, fan, and related components positioned above the print bed

entities.name: LoadCell technology
entities.description: Technology measuring distance between nozzle and bed with high accuracy

entities.name: Print bed
entities.description:
Surface on which the print is deposited, with calibration grid pattern
The surface on which the object is printed, shown with grid pattern in slicer preview.

entities.name: First layer quality
entities.description: Observable quality of the initial printed layer, used for inspection

entities.name: Plastic peeling off bed
entities.description: First layer failing to adhere to bed, plastic peels off

entities.name: Grease traces on bed
entities.description: Contamination on bed surface causing poor adhesion

entities.name: Stop print
entities.description: Action of stopping the print via the screen icon when plastic peels off

entities.name: Clean the bed
entities.description: Cleaning the print bed surface before retrying a print

entities.name: First Print Troubleshooting chapter
entities.description: Reference chapter with tips for repeated first print failures

entities.name: Observe first layer
aliases: Carefully observe the quality of the first layer
entities.description: Task of visually inspecting first layer quality during printing

entities.name: Living Hinge
entities.description: A thin flexible connecting section between two printed parts (e.g., box base and lid) that allows movement without a separate hinge component.

entities.name: Living Hinge Box Design
aliases: Design process for living hinge box
entities.description: Design procedure for creating a box with an integrated living hinge by extruding a rectangular connecting shape between base and lid.

entities.name: Box Closed Height
entities.description: The total height of the example box once closed, measured from base to surface, given as 16 mm.

entities.name: Hinge Flex Clearance
entities.description: The distance/clearance needed around the living hinge to allow it to flex without breaking or excessive bending stress.

entities.name: Prusa Academy Course
aliases: Design Principles for 3D Printed Parts course
entities.description: An online Prusa Academy course covering design principles for 3D printed parts, including a section on living hinges (topic 3.9).

entities.name: Infill
entities.description: Parameter controlling internal geometric structure density of a printed object, affecting speed, strength, and appearance.

entities.name: Infill Pattern
entities.description: Geometric shape used to fill object interior, e.g. grids, hexagons, diagonal cross-hatch.

entities.name: PrusaSlicer
entities.description: Slicing software used to configure and preview print settings such as infill and brim.

entities.name: Support Material
entities.description: Structures printed under overhanging areas of a model to support them during printing, shown in green in preview.

entities.name: Brim
entities.description: A wider first layer printed around the model's base to increase bed adhesion and reduce warping; enabled via a checkbox in PrusaSlicer.

entities.name: Enable Brim
entities.description: Task of enabling the brim function in PrusaSlicer by checking the 'Brim' box in the right column menu.

entities.name: Remove Brim
entities.description: After printing, the brim can be removed by hand or with a knife/scalpel.

entities.name: Warping
entities.description: Failure mode where the printed object's base lifts or deforms due to poor bed adhesion; reduced by using a brim.

entities.name: Bed Adhesion
entities.description: Degree to which the first printed layer sticks to the print bed, improved by brim.

entities.name: Repair walkthrough
entities.description: A video walkthrough of a repair/maintenance procedure for this product.

edges.description: The print sheet must be installed onto the printer before the calibration can be performed.
Internet setup configuration is the first step performed within the Selftest wizard.
The wizard displays its steps and prompts on the LCD touchscreen.
The manual selftest option is found under Control in the LCD menu.
Running Full Selftest manually restarts the Selftest (Calibration Wizard) process.
Selftest checks for and helps identify basic assembly issues on kit-built printers.
Selftest helps identify issues caused by rough handling during shipping on pre-assembled printers.
Toolhead equipped with LoadCell technology for nozzle-to-bed measurement
LoadCell measures distance accurately affecting first layer quality
Grease traces on bed may cause print not to hold well, resulting in peeling
If plastic is peeling, stop the print using the Stop print icon
After stopping print, clean the bed before trying again
Cleaning the bed addresses the cause of peeling and allows retry
Observing first layer quality helps detect peeling issue
If first print fails repeatedly, consult troubleshooting chapter
Toolhead positioned above the print bed during printing
The living hinge is the key feature designed within the box design procedure.
The box design must account for the total closed height of 16mm to leave room for the hinge.
Sufficient distance must be allowed in the design for the hinge to flex without breaking.
The flex clearance setting affects how the living hinge performs mechanically.
The course includes a topic (3.9 Compliance and living hinges) covering this design element.
The course topic on living hinges illustrates this box design example.
Infill setting determines which geometric pattern is used inside the object.
Infill density/pattern is configured within PrusaSlicer.
Support structures are printed on the build plate beneath overhanging model areas.
Enabling brim increases adhesion to the bed.
Brim reduces the risk of warping by increasing bed adhesion.
Enabling the brim function activates the brim setting in PrusaSlicer.
Brim is enabled by checking a box within the PrusaSlicer interface.
After printing finishes, the brim is removed from the model.
Brim is printed as a flat extension around the model's footprint on the print bed.
